title: Current generation architecture for an implantable medical device
abstract: An implantable pulse generator (IPG) is disclosed having a plurality of electrode nodes, each electrode node configured to be coupled to an electrode to provide stimulation pulses to a patient's tissue. The IPG includes a digital-to-analog converter configured to amplify a reference current to a first current specified by first control signals; a first resistance configured to receive the first current, wherein a voltage across the first resistance is held to a reference voltage at a first node; a plurality of branches each comprising a second resistance and configured to produce a branch current, wherein a voltage across each second resistance is held to the reference voltage at second nodes; and a switch matrix configurable to selectively couple any branch current to any of the electrode nodes via the second nodes.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS; 1. A pulse generator, comprising: a plurality of electrode nodes, each electrode node configured to be coupled to an electrode to provide stimulation pulses to a patient's tissue; a digital-to-analog converter configured to amplify a reference current to a first current specified by first control signals; a first resistance configured to receive the first current, wherein a voltage across the first resistance is held to a reference voltage at a first node; a plurality of branches each comprising a second resistance and configured to produce a branch current, wherein a voltage across each second resistance is held to the reference voltage at second nodes; and a switch matrix configurable to selectively couple any branch current to any of the electrode nodes via the second nodes. 2. The pulse generator of claim 1 , wherein the first resistance comprises one or more first transistors, and each second resistance comprises a second transistor. 3. The pulse generator of claim 2, wherein gates of the one or more first transistors are coupled to gates of each second transistor. 4. The pulse generator of claim 3, wherein the first resistance comprises a plurality of first transistors, and wherein the first resistance is selectively formed by one or more second control signals that place some number of the first transistors in parallel. 5. The pulse generator of any of claims 1 -4, further comprising a reference resistance, wherein the reference voltage is generated by passing the reference current though the reference resistor. 6. The pulse generator of any of claims 1-5, further comprising a first operational amplifier, wherein the reference voltage and the first node are input to the operational amplifier. 7. The pulse generator of claim 6, wherein an output of the first operational amplifier controls the first resistance and each second resistance. 8. The pulse generator of any of claims 1-7, further comprising a plurality of second operational amplifiers and a plurality of output transistors each controlled by an output of one of the second operational amplifiers, wherein one of the output transistors intervenes between one of the second nodes and one of the electrode nodes. 9. The pulse generator of claim 8, wherein one input of each of the second operational amplifiers is connected to the first node, and wherein one input of each of the second operational amplifiers is connected to one of the second nodes. 10. The pulse generator of any of claims 1 -9, wherein the first resistance has a first width, and wherein each second resistance has a second width, and wherein each second resistance differs from the first resistance by a factor of the first width divided by the second width. 11. The pulse generator of any of claims 1 -10, further comprising at least one implantable lead, wherein the electrodes are located on the lead. 12. A pulse generator, comprising: a plurality of electrode nodes, each electrode node configured to be coupled to an electrode to provide stimulation pulses to a patient's tissue; a first current configured to mirror a reference current though a reference resistor to generate a reference voltage; a first resistance configured to receive a first current, wherein a voltage across the first resistance is held to the reference voltage at a first node; a plurality of branches each comprising a second resistance and configured to produce a branch current, wherein a voltage across each second resistance is held to the reference voltage at second nodes; and a switch matrix configurable to selectively couple any branch current to any of the electrode nodes via the second nodes. 13. The pulse generator of claim 12, wherein the first resistance comprises one or more first transistors, and each second resistance comprises a second transistor. 14. The pulse generator of claim 13, wherein gates of the one or more first transistors are coupled to gates of each second transistor. 15. The pulse generator of claim 14, wherein the first resistance comprises a plurality of first transistors, and wherein the first resistance is selectively formed by one or more control signals that place some number of the first transistors in parallel. 16. The pulse generator of any of claims 12-15, further comprising a digital-to-analog converter configured to amplify the reference current to the first current. 17. The pulse generator of claim 16, further comprising a second current mirror, wherein the second current mirror is configured to amplify the reference current to the first current. 18. The pulse generator of any of claims 12-17, further comprising a first operational amplifier, wherein the reference voltage and the first node are input to the operational amplifier, wherein an output of the first operational amplifier controls the first resistance and each second resistance. 19. The pulse generator of any of claims 12-18, further comprising a plurality of second operational amplifiers and a plurality of output transistors each controlled by an output of one of the second operational amplifiers, wherein one of the output transistors intervenes between one of the second nodes and one of the electrode nodes. 20. The pulse generator of claim 19, wherein one input of each of the second operational amplifiers is connected to the first node, and wherein one input of each of the second operational amplifiers is connected to one of the second nodes. 21. The pulse generator of any of claims 12-20, wherein the first resistance has a first width, and wherein each second resistance has a second width, and wherein each second resistance differs from the first resistance by a factor of the first width divided by the second width. 22. The pulse generator of any of claims 12-21, further comprising at least one implantable lead, wherein the electrodes are located on the lead.
